scope: Primary-source evidence for advanced Gmsh controls and COMSOL-inspired mesh workflow

local_validation.case: Two conformal fragmented box volumes with a Constant field restricted by VolumesList and combined with a coarse field using Min

sources.title: Gmsh 4.15.2 Reference Manual
evidence: Mesh size is the minimum of point, curvature, background-field, and per-entity constraints, bounded by global min/max and scale factor.
Distance, Threshold, Box, MathEval, Extend, Min, Max, Restrict, PostView and BoundaryLayer fields are available.
Restrict can target point, curve, surface, or volume entity lists.
model.mesh.setSize currently handles only point entities.
model.mesh.refine uniformly splits the current model mesh.
Extrude with Layers creates structured 3D meshes and Recombine can produce prisms, hexahedra, or pyramids.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: The Mesh Node
evidence: Mesh sequences can be physics-controlled or user-controlled.
User-controlled defaults are Size plus Free Triangular in 2D or Free Tetrahedral in 3D.
Physics-controlled sequences are regenerated from active physics and are distinct from solution-error adaptation.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Size
evidence: Size attributes can target entire geometry, domains, boundaries, edges, or points through manual or named selections.
Controls include maximum/minimum size, growth rate, curvature factor, and narrow-region resolution.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Meshing Operations and Attributes
evidence: Generator nodes include Free Triangular, Free Quad, Mapped, Free Tetrahedral, Swept, Boundary Layers, Edge, and Vertex.
Attributes include Size, Size Expression, Distribution, Boundary Layer Properties, Corner Refinement, and Identical Mesh.
Modifying operations include Refine, Adapt, and Convert.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Refine
evidence: Refine can target domain, boundary, or 3D edge selections.
Regular refinement and longest-side splitting are available, including bounding-box restriction.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Modifying the Element Size Using Refine and Adapt
evidence: Refine splits existing elements; Adapt can refine and coarsen based on error estimate, absolute size, or isotropic metric.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Boundary Layers
evidence: Boundary layers are selection-driven and support corner splitting/trimming and smooth transition to the interior.
2D uses layered quads; 3D uses prisms or hexahedra according to the boundary mesh.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Free Tetrahedral
evidence: Free Tetrahedral fills selected or remaining domains and can bridge adjacent quad faces with pyramids.
Size, Size Expression, Distribution, and Corner Refinement attributes control the generator.

sources.title: COMSOL 6.3: Mesh Element Quality
evidence: COMSOL exposes skewness, maximum-angle, volume/circumradius, volume/length, condition-number, growth-rate, and curved-skewness measures.
